evidence: The term 'artless' strongly resonates with scale.individual (10) as it describes a personal lack of refinement. It connects to ontological.semantic (5) as it directly addresses cultural value. Symmetry.destructive (4) reflects its negative connotation as a flaw. Resonance.essential (5) captures its core essence of lacking culture, while ethical (3) highlights its moral judgment aspect.

Scores peak in symmetry.destructive (7) due to the term's negative connotation, scale.individual (7) as it describes a person's lack of refinement, and value_generation.ethical (6) reflecting moral judgment. Resonance.essential (4) and ontological.semantic (5) highlight its core meaning tied to cultural values. Most slots score 0 or low due to the term's specificity to individual moral-cultural critique.

Barbarian strongly resonates with its core essence (resonance.essential), moral judgment (value_generation.ethical), and relational dynamics (ontological.relational). It operates at societal scales (scale.society), catalyzes change (interconnection.catalytic), and reflects a destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) to culture. The term is identified categorically (epistemological.identification) and tied to material lack (ontological.material).

Barbaric strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (10) and value_generation.ethical (10), as it represents a moral judgment against cultural refinement. It also aligns with ontological.semantic (9) due to its evaluative nature and scale.society (9) when describing uncivilized societal norms. Scores are low elsewhere as the term is focused on cultural deficiency rather than systemic or existential dimensions.

Barbarity resonates most with symmetry.destructive (7) due to its association with the absence of culture's civilizing influence. It also aligns with value_generation.ethical (7) as it inherently involves moral judgment. Moderate scores reflect its relational impact (ontological.relational/structural), contingent nature (lawfulness.contingent), and superficial/structural resonance.

Crude (uncultured) strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (7), semantic ontology (7), and individual scale (7). It also aligns with identification (6), ethical value (6), and essential resonance (6). Perception (5), experience (5), and predictable lawfulness (5) show moderate connections.

Crudeness strongly resonates with its essential core (resonance.essential 9), destructive nature (symmetry.destructive 10), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical 7). It manifests as a persistent individual trait (temporal.continuation 5, scale.individual 7) and is perceived through observable behavior (epistemological.perception 6). Its predictable presence (lawfulness.predictable 5) and relational impact (ontological.relational 5) further ground its semantic resonance.

Crudity strongly resonates with individual lack of refinement (scale.individual), identification as uncultured (epistemological.identification), its core essence in meaning (ontological.semantic), destructive pathology (symmetry.destructive), ethical judgment (value_generation.ethical), and touching the core essence (resonance.essential). Lower scores reflect its transient, sensory-perceived nature and lack of systemic or existential depth.

The highest scores reflect its association with societal structures (scale.society, scale.community), moral judgment (value_generation.ethical), and semantic opposition to WLD.culture (ontological.semantic). Lower scores highlight its perceptible traits (epistemological.perception) and negative relational dynamics (symmetry.destructive).

The word resonates most strongly with symmetry.destructive (5) as it opposes WLD.culture, value_generation.ethical (4) due to moral judgments about refinement, and resonance.essential (5) as it touches the core essence of lacking culture. It also aligns with scale.community (4) and epistemological.identification (5), reflecting its use to label groups or individuals as lacking refinement.

The word 'rough' most strongly resonates with the semantic essence of cultural deficiency (ontological.semantic 5), destructive opposition to refinement (symmetry.destructive 5), and direct identification of an uncultured state (epistemological.identification 5). It also ties to individual-level manifestations (scale.individual 5) and the core essence of lacking refinement (resonance.essential 5).

Roughness in the context of WLD.uncultured strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (8) as it represents a lack of cultural refinement. It also aligns with ontological.semantic (7) due to its connection to meaning and value judgments about culture. High scores in scale.individual (7) and scale.community (5) reflect its application to people and groups. Ethical value (5) highlights the moral dimension of being uncultured, while perception (5) and identification (6) reflect direct sensory and categorical recognition of roughness.

The term 'savage' strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (as the antithesis of culture), ontological semantic value (carrying moral judgment), and ethical implications. It is identified through direct labeling (epistemological.identification) and touches on essential aspects of a person's perceived essence.

The highest scores reflect 'savage' as an individual lacking refinement (scale.individual=10), its identification as a cultural opposite (epistemological.identification=5), and its core essence contrasting with WLD.culture (resonance.essential=5). Ethical judgment (value.ethical=3) and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive=3) further highlight its opposition to cultured norms.

Savageness strongly resonates with ethical value (5) and symmetry.destructive (5) as the dark counterpart to culture. It also ties to semantic meaning (5), individual scale (5), and relational dynamics (2). Lower scores reflect its transient influence (temporal slots) and limited ethical/moral implications beyond judgment.

The word 'tasteless' in this context strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (as a cultural pathology) and value_generation.ethical (judging lack of refinement as morally lacking). It also aligns with ontological.semantic (operating on meaning/value layers) and scale.individual (primarily describing personal lack of refinement).

Uncivilised strongly resonates with scales (individual/community/society) as it describes a lack of culture across these levels. It aligns with identification (naming the absence of refinement) and semantic meaning (moral/cultural value judgments). Its destructive symmetry opposes WLD.culture's constructive nature, and ethical value underscores its moral dimension.

The word 'uncivilized' strongly resonates with the semantic layer (ontological.semantic:5) because it directly addresses values and norms. It also reflects destructive dynamics (symmetry.destructive:5) as the antithesis of culture. Ethical judgment (value_generation.ethical:5) is central to its meaning, as it often carries moral implications about societal refinement.

Uncultivated strongly resonates with individual and societal scales (5/3/2), reflecting a lack of refinement in persons or systems. It ties to ethical judgment (4) and aesthetic evaluation (3), and its destructive symmetry (5) underscores its negative valuation. The term's semantic (5) and structural (4) resonance reflects its role in defining cultural value systems.

Uncultured strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (as a deficiency opposite to culture), scale.individual (applies to persons), ontological.semantic (relates to values/judgment), and value_generation.ethical (moral dimension). Lower scores reflect weak ties to relational dynamics, superficial traits, and identification/naming processes.

The highest scores reflect 'unprocessed' as a categorizable state (epistemological.identification), its value judgment tied to cultural deficiency (ontological.semantic), and its association with negative dynamics (symmetry.destructive). Lower scores relate to its predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical).

Unrefined resonates most strongly with epistemological categories (perception, identification, understanding) as it describes observable states. It applies to individuals and communities (scale) and touches on essential aspects of cultural value (resonance.essential). Its destructive symmetry with culture and contingent nature (dependent on conditions) further clarify its resonance.

The strongest resonances are with individual assessment (scale.individual), labeling (epistemological.identification), semantic value judgments (ontological.semantic), destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical). These reflect how 'unskilled' in the context of lacking culture critiques personal capability and societal norms, while temporal.influence (3) shows its impact on cultural trajectories over time.

The highest scores reflect 'wild' as a value judgment (ontological.semantic), a core essence of lacking refinement (resonance.essential), and its destructive contrast to culture (symmetry.destructive). It strongly applies to individuals (scale.individual) and involves categorization (epistemological.identification). Lower scores align with its persistence over time (temporal.continuation) and moral undertones (value.ethical).

The highest scores reflect 'wild' as a value judgment (semantic.5) tied to individual lack of refinement (scale.individual.5) and ethical implications (value.ethical.5). The destructive symmetry (3) highlights its negative connotation opposite to culture. Resonance.essential (5) captures its core critique of cultural norms.

Wildness (as uncultured) strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (its negative, uncivilized aspect), value.ethical (judgment of moral inferiority), and resonance.essential (touching core human nature). It also aligns with ontological.semantic (its meaning as lacking refinement) and scale.individual/community (manifesting in personal or group behavior).
